---
title: "2.11 Nested Iteration"
subtitle: "Part 2: Turtle Nested Loops and Snowflakes"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Nested Loops with Turtles

### A snowflake design

Try nested loops with turtles to create a snowflake design.

The turtle below is trying to draw a square many times to create a snowflake pattern.

Textbook prompt: Can you change the outer loop so that the pattern completes all the way around?

Try different ending values for the counter `i` to find the smallest number that works between `5` and `15`.

## Code Task

### `activecode:: TurtleNestedLoop`

To make the drawing faster, you can call the `World` or `Turtle` object's `setSpeed` method with a `0-100` delay value where `0` is the fastest.

Datafile: `turtleClasses.jar`

## Code Window

### `activecode:: TurtleNestedLoop`

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
import java.awt.*;
import java.util.*;

public class TurtleDrawSnowflake
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        World world = new World(300, 300);
        Turtle yertle = new Turtle(world);
        yertle.setSpeed(25); // fast 0 - 100 slow
        yertle.setColor(Color.blue);

        // Outer loop will do inner loop to draw a square,
        // and then turn a little each time
        // How many times should the outer loop run to complete the pattern? Try
        // some numbers between 5 and 15.
        for (int i = 1; i <= 5; i++)
        {

            // inner loop draws a square
            for (int sides = 1; sides <= 4; sides++)
            {
                yertle.forward();
                yertle.turn(90);
            }
            // turn a little before drawing square again
            yertle.turn(30);
        }
        world.show(true);
    }
}
```
:::

## Test Requirements

### `activecode:: TurtleNestedLoop`

Runestone checks that the outer loop condition contains a number high enough to complete the drawing.

The checker looks for an `i <= ?;` pattern and expects the parsed ending value to be at least `12`.

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Turtle Snowflakes

In the last exercise, you used nested for-loops to have the turtle draw a square repeatedly to make a snowflake.

Use the Active Code window below to have `yertle` draw shapes using nested loops.

Textbook note: We encourage you to work in pairs on this.

## Challenge Steps

### Tasks 1-2

::: {.tight-list}
1. Complete the code to draw a snowflake of triangles. Triangles have 3 sides and you will need to turn 120 degrees, external angle, 3 times to draw the triangle. Use the `turnAmount` variable for the single turn after drawing a triangle. Try changing `turnAmount` to `40` to see how many times you need to loop with a wider distance between the triangles.
2. Change the outer loop so that it runs the number of times needed by using a formula with the `turnAmount` variable and `360`. Can you draw a snowflake using more or less triangles than before by just changing the `turnAmount` value?
:::

## Challenge Steps

### Tasks 3-5

::: {.tight-list}
3. Create another variable called `n` for the number of sides in the polygon the inner loop draws. Change the angle in the inner loop to also use a formula with `360` and this new variable. Can you change your snowflake to draw squares or pentagons instead?
4. Add an `if`/`else` statement that changes the color of the pen before the inner loop depending on whether the outer loop variable is odd or even.
5. Be creative and design a unique snowflake.
:::

## Code Task

### `activecode:: challenge-Turtle-Nested-Loop-Snowflakes`

Textbook prompt: Use nested for-loops to have the turtle draw a snowflake of polygons.

Use the variable `turnAmount` to turn after each shape and the variable `n` for the sides of the polygon.

Datafile: `turtleClasses.jar`

## Code Window

### `activecode:: challenge-Turtle-Nested-Loop-Snowflakes`

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
import java.awt.*;
import java.util.*;

public class TurtleSnowflakes
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        World world = new World(300, 300);
        Turtle yertle = new Turtle(world);
        yertle.setSpeed(25); // fast 0 - 100 slow
        yertle.setColor(Color.blue);

        // Use this variable in the loops
        int turnAmount = 30;

        // 1. Write a for loop that runs many times
        // 2. Change it to use turnAmount to figure out how many times to run

        // 1 & 2. Write an inner loop that draws a triangle (3 sides, 120 degree
        // turns)
        // 3. Then change it to be any polygon with a variable n

        // turn turnAmount degrees before drawing the polygon again

        // 4. Add an if statement that changes the colors depending on the loop
        // variables

        world.show(true);
    }
}
```
:::

## Focus Block

### Outer and inner loops

The outer loop repeats the shape around the full 360-degree turn.

The inner loop draws one polygon.

```java
int turnAmount = 30;

// outer loop count should use 360/turnAmount

// inner loop draws triangle first, then any n-sided polygon
```

## Test Requirements

### `activecode:: challenge-Turtle-Nested-Loop-Snowflakes`

Runestone checks that the submitted code includes:

::: {.tight-list}
- 2 `for` loops, nested
- an `if` statement to change colors
- only 1 Turtle forward call
- only 2 Turtle turn calls
- the expression `360/turnAmount`
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- explain that the inner loop draws one square, triangle, or polygon
- explain that the outer loop repeats that shape around the center
- use `turnAmount` to control the spacing between repeated shapes
- use `360/turnAmount` to calculate how many outer-loop iterations are needed
- use `n` to generalize the number of polygon sides
- place the color-changing `if` before the inner loop
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
